text:
Once upon a time, in a land filled with wonder and flowers that sang melodies at dusk, there lived a tiny fairy named Flora. Flora didn't look like your typical fairy—she was only as wide as the lash of a spiderweb. Despite her small size, Flora was the most resilient being in the kingdom of Everglow.

One misty morning, Flora found an old, rickety shoe at the edge of the woods. It was old, perhaps centuries-old, and smelled faintly of dust and forgotten dreams. She picked it up, feeling its weight, and decided it needed a home. The shoe seemed to welcome her—a sort of forgotten heirloom that acknowledged its forgotten possessor. From that day on, Flora called it Echo.

The shoe had mysterious straps and buckles. It was said that every time someone put their foot in it, they would whisper secrets into Echo's leather lining. Flora listened eagerly, recording whispered promises, lost loves
